description: Payload for <!-- phase-containment-{ID} --> HTML comment YAML blocks. Records per-finding phase-containment metadata for escape-rate ledger tracking. Governed by the calibration-pipeline skill.

finding_key.description: Uniform cross-surface identifier: {surface}:{stable_finding_key}. Surface is one of: code-review, design-challenge, plan-stress-test, post-review-observer.

introduced_phase.description: The phase in which the defect was introduced.

catchable_phase.description: The earliest phase in which the defect could have been caught.

caught_stage.description: The stage at which the defect was actually caught.

escape_distance.description: Computed as: projection(caught_stage) - ordinal(catchable_phase). Must match the recomputed value or the entry is rejected.

severity.description: Finding severity. MUST match routing-config.json:378 severity enum. Drift is verified by phase-containment-core.Tests.ps1 enum drift test.

systemic_fix_type.description: Type of systemic fix required. MUST match routing-config.json:397 systemic_fix_type enum. Drift is verified by phase-containment-core.Tests.ps1 enum drift test.

category.description: Finding category. MUST match routing-config.json:386 category enum. Drift is verified by phase-containment-core.Tests.ps1 enum drift test.

apparatus_meta.description: When true, this entry is excluded from all rate numerators, denominators, and n counts. Use for seeded baseline entries that are part of the apparatus, not organic findings.

seed.description: When true, distinguishes seeded baseline entries from organic entries. Seeded entries are known injected defects used to calibrate the pipeline.

appended_at.description: Optional. Block-level re-annotation timestamp (strict ISO-8601 UTC, Z-suffixed) written when a block is edited in place. Lets dedup (Invoke-PhaseContainmentDedup) correctly identify the freshest copy of a re-annotated finding_key even when the edit lands in a comment whose own createdAt does not advance (GitHub does not bump createdAt on PATCH). Falls back to the comment-level createdAt when absent, so historic blocks are unaffected. This pattern is NOT enforced at runtime by any schema validator — phase-containment-rolling-history-core.ps1 performs its own runtime strict-format check and routes a malformed-but-present value into InvalidEntryCount rather than silently reverting to first-seen.
